allocation_status: withheld: model_portfolio must record an exact identity

source_sections.references: [Li76] Lin, S., On two problems of Erd\H{o}s concerning sums of distinct factorials. Bell Laboratories internal memorandum (1960).
source_sections.remarks: See also [403]. Lin \cite{Li76} has shown that $f(2,2) \leq 254$.

subproblem_targets.focus_question: For which integers $a\geq 1$ and primes $p$ is there a finite upper bound on those $k$ such that there are $a=a_1<\cdots<a_n$ with\[p^k \mid (a_1!+\cdots+a_n!)?
\]If $f(a,p)$ is the greatest such $k$, how does this function behave?
Is there a prime $p$ and an infinite sequence $a_1<a_2<\cdots$ such that if $p^{m_k}$ is the highest power of $p$ dividing $\sum_{i\leq k}a_i!$ then $m_k\to \infty$?

Is there a prime $p$ and an infinite sequence $a_1<a_2<\cdots$ such that if $p^{m_k}$ is the highest power of $p$ dividing $\sum_{i\leq k}a_i!$ then $m_k\to \infty$?
